name: Airbnb Photography Guide For Holiday Let And Rental Hosts

step.name: Prepare the room before the camera comes out
step.text: Clear every worktop, bedside table, and sofa cushion of clutter. Make the bed taut, fluff the pillows, and align every lampshade. Open all curtains and blinds fully.

step.name: Set up the tripod at chair height
step.text: Tripod at roughly 1.2 to 1.4 metres. Use a bubble level or phone level screen to get the camera perfectly vertical.

step.name: Turn on every internal light
step.text: Every lamp, every overhead, every under-cabinet strip. Warm pools of interior light signal 'cosy' in a thumbnail.

step.name: Compose from a corner diagonally opposite the main feature
step.text: The main feature is usually the window, fireplace, or best piece of furniture. Shooting from the opposite corner gives depth and leads the eye.

step.name: Set exposure for the window, not the room
step.text: A blown-out window makes a room look dark and small. Expose so the window retains detail, lift shadows in edit.

step.name: Take three frames at slightly different angles
step.text: One at your chosen angle, one a step to either side. Insurance against reflections or bad lines discovered in edit.
